intro: Your laptop opens. The notifications are already piling up.

scenarios.title: The Monday Morning
scenarios.subtitle: Where do you start?
scenarios.prompt: You've got a full week ahead. Meetings, deadlines, and a few things you've been putting off. Where do you start?

choices.text: Open my calendar and map out the whole week before touching anything else.
Start with whatever's due first and work forward from there.
Open my inbox. That's where the fires are.
Pick something easy to knock out and build momentum.

scenarios.title: The Difficult Email
scenarios.subtitle: We've all stared at a draft for way too long.
scenarios.prompt: There's an email you've been putting off. You need to push back on a deadline and you know it's not going to land well.

choices.text: Write it carefully, reread it a few times, then finally hit send.
choices.reaction: Draft four. This one's the one. Maybe.

choices.text: Type it up quick and send it before I overthink it.
choices.reaction: Sent. No taking it back now.

choices.text: Slack a coworker first. "Can you read this before I send it?"
Close the tab. I'll deal with it after lunch.

scenarios.title: The Meeting Aftermath
scenarios.subtitle: What happens after matters more than the meeting.
scenarios.prompt: You just got out of an hour-long meeting. There were action items, a few decisions, and something you're pretty sure you're now responsible for.

choices.text: Sit down and organize my notes before I forget anything.
I'll remember what matters. No need to write everything down.
Check the sticky note I scribbled on during the call. That's enough.
Wait, what did I agree to do?

scenarios.title: The Skill Gap
scenarios.subtitle: There's always something new.
scenarios.prompt: Your manager mentions a tool the team is starting to use. You've never touched it. Everyone else seems to already know it.

choices.text: Go home and look up tutorials that night.
Figure it out when I actually need to use it.
Ask a coworker to walk me through it.
Nod like I know what they're talking about and hope it comes together.

scenarios.title: The Opportunity
scenarios.subtitle: When there's a chance to step up.
scenarios.prompt: Wednesday. There's a problem the team is stuck on and someone brings it up in a meeting. You have an idea but it's outside your usual lane.

choices.text: Speak up. Even if it's not fully formed, it's worth throwing out.
Stay quiet but write it up after and send it to my manager.
choices.reaction: The email drafts itself on the walk back.

choices.text: Wait to see if someone else says something similar first.
Keep it to myself. I don't have enough expertise to back it up.

scenarios.title: The Repetitive Task
scenarios.subtitle: Every. Single. Week.
scenarios.prompt: Thursday morning. That weekly report is due again. Same format, same process, same everything. Every single week.

choices.text: I've got a template that makes it fast. Knock it out in 20 minutes.
Grind through it like I always do.
Rush through it. Nobody reads these anyway.
It's sitting in my to-do list from last week. I'll get to it.
choices.reaction: Now it's sitting there from two weeks ago.

scenarios.title: The Afternoon Chaos
scenarios.subtitle: Things are piling up.
scenarios.prompt: Thursday afternoon. You've got 14 unread emails, three Slack threads you lost track of, and a tab open from this morning you forgot about.

choices.text: Take 15 minutes to clear the clutter and get organized.
It's messy but I know where everything is. Mostly.
Something in there is going to bite me. I just don't know what yet.
choices.reaction: The question is when, not if.

choices.text: Close everything and start fresh tomorrow. I'm done.

scenarios.title: The End of the Week
scenarios.subtitle: When work is over, is it really over?
scenarios.prompt: Friday evening. You close your laptop. The week is over.

choices.text: Open my personal to-do list. There's a whole other list waiting.
Keep thinking about something I didn't finish.
Feel behind but couldn't tell you on what exactly.
Shut it off. The weekend is mine.
